subject: Sociology
title: Sociology and Health
description: A quick win: understand Sociology and Health.

hook.text: Your zip code predicts your life expectancy better than your genetic code. Health is a social issue, not just medical.

buildup.text: Medical sociology studies how social factors — race, class, gender — shape who gets sick and who gets care.

discovery.text: The social determinants of health include income, housing, education, and neighborhood. Medicine alone can't fix them.

twist.text: In the U.S., wealthy Americans live 10-15 years longer than the poorest. The gap isn't genes — it's resources.

climax.text: Treating disease without addressing social conditions is like mopping the floor while the faucet runs.

punchline.text: Where you live matters more than what medicine you take.

quiz.question: What are social determinants of health?
options: Factors like income, housing, and education that shape health outcomes
Types of medicine prescribed by doctors
